user_symptom: customers may have experienced difficulties connecting to resources. Impacted customers may have experienced timeouts or 401 authorization errors
root_cause: a pending (deployed, but awaiting reboot) OS security update which had introduced a networking change on the updated clusters... the machines lost network connectivity and could no longer handle requests

user_symptom: intermittent errors while accessing or performing service management operations - such as create, update, delete - for multiple resources
root_cause: misconfiguration in the broad phase of a deployment for ARM services, which resulted in unanticipated utilization of a single partition of Cosmos DB

user_symptom: issues attempting control plane operations including create, update and delete operations on resources
root_cause: a backend Cosmos DB was misconfigured to block legitimate access from Azure Resource Manager (ARM)

user_symptom: service connection failures or possible timeouts
root_cause: A specific network traffic pattern combined with a networking stack configuration on the SQL Gateway instances triggered an imbalance on the CPU processing of new connection requests

user_symptom: Connections to Cosmos DB accounts in this region may have resulted in an error or timeout.
root_cause: Frontend load balancing procedure had a regression that did not factor in the effect of a reduction in available cluster capacity, due to ongoing maintenance.

user_symptom: difficulties connecting to resources, timeouts or 401 authorization errors
root_cause: OS security update introduced unsupported networking change

user_symptom: customers may have experienced issues accessing or using Azure, Microsoft 365 and Power Platform services
root_cause: utility power sag... which tripped a subset of the cooling system chiller units

user_symptom: Multiple Azure services experienced availability, connectivity, and service management failures due to storage availability issues
root_cause: Incomplete VM host network address allow list update for storage scale units, causing disk read/write request rejections

user_symptom: customers may have experienced failures when trying to access Azure SQL Database and Data Warehouse resources, new connections to databases in these regions may have resulted in an error or timeout
root_cause: a recent maintenance activity did not complete successfully, gateways to hold an incorrect certificate configuration that effectively blocked connections

user_symptom: Connections to Azure Cosmos DB accounts in this region may have resulted in an error or timeout
root_cause: Configuration change to Azure Load Balancer resulted in network connectivity issues and high CPU utilization

user_symptom: customers may have experienced connectivity and service availability errors while accessing their Cosmos DB databases
root_cause: timer callback on one of the nodes was fired multiple times at a frequency higher than intended

user_symptom: Read and write requests failed for West US, increased latency observed
root_cause: Code regression in Cosmos DB deployment triggered a rare failure condition in configuration store

user_symptom: Data ingestion and routing failures for Log Analytics and Sentinel
root_cause: Misconfigured code deployment causing service restart loops

service_name: Azure SQL DB and Cosmos DB
user_symptom: database unavailability and may have seen errors when connecting to their database instances
root_cause: Race condition in RPC mechanism causing certificate rotation failure

user_symptom: Database connectivity errors, service management operation issues, autoscaling and metric loading problems
root_cause: Memory hardware failure in network router during automated maintenance, causing control plane/data plane synchronization process failure

user_symptom: unresponsiveness and startup failures on Windows machines
root_cause: CrowdStrike antivirus software update

user_symptom: dropping around 10% of traffic, and causing high latency for services traversing the network
root_cause: programming error caused network congestion, incompatible FIB push caused router failures

user_symptom: degraded performance, network drops, or timeouts when accessing Azure resources
root_cause: routing protocol metric change within an ISP backbone network

user_symptom: increased latency and intermittent connectivity issues
root_cause: single router hardware fault in Regional Network Gateway

user_symptom: customers experienced issues with network connectivity, manifesting as long network latency and/or timeouts when attempting to connect to resources hosted in Azure regions
root_cause: Unqualified network configuration change during a maintenance task, specifically a command to purge IGP database that behaved differently across router manufacturers

user_symptom: Network connections resulted in connection failure, two-thirds of new connection attempts impacted
root_cause: Configuration error pushed to routers in Microsoft Wide Area Network in Singapore and Amsterdam

user_symptom: Intermittent network failures, packet loss, connection delays
root_cause: Router in London dropping network packets on one line card

user_symptom: a subset of customers experienced issues connecting to their services
root_cause: incorrect IPv4 network prefix applied during router update

user_symptom: connectivity issues such as network drops, latency, and/or degradation when attempting to access or manage Azure resources
root_cause: sudden and significant increase of traffic, upwards of 60 Tbps in additional traffic compared to the normal levels

service_name: Azure Front Door and Azure CDN
location: Japan East, Japan West, Korea South, Korea Central, West US
user_symptom: intermittent HTTPS request connectivity failures
root_cause: A code defect in the rolled-out version resulted in a race condition where a few servers in the impacted locations tried to revert to a full TLS handshake

user_symptom: Error notifications during service management operations
root_cause: Data store resource limit exhaustion in a partition

user_symptom: Up to 50% of customer requests failed, issues performing resource management operations
root_cause: Positive feedback loop in ARM web API tier due to new cache implementation and internal service traffic

user_symptom: Customers experienced error notifications and issues accessing Azure Portal
root_cause: Layer 7 distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks overwhelming origin servers

user_symptom: intermittent network failures and packet loss while trying to connect to resources behind a Software Load Balancer
root_cause: a change in our latest code deployment which caused some MUX instances to store the backend DIPs in a different order

user_symptom: Delays in long running control plane operations, timeouts and operation failures
root_cause: Certificate rotation exposed a latent bug in the update process which caused the processes to begin crashing

user_symptom: Connectivity issues, VM offline, database and service degradation
root_cause: UPS and power infrastructure failure, BIOS software bug

user_symptom: customers may have experienced issues connecting to resources hosted in this region
root_cause: an ice storm in the region resulted in a loss of power to both primary and secondary utility power feeds

user_symptom: difficulties connecting to resources, services experienced impact, control plane issues
root_cause: unplanned power oscillation, electrical transients, AHUs detecting a potential fault and shutting down

user_symptom: increased latency or difficulties connecting to Azure services
root_cause: multiple fiber-cut event isolated the Stockholm and Helsinki Azure Edge sites

user_symptom: intermittent connection errors, timeouts, or latency spikes
root_cause: network misconfiguration caused congestion and packet loss

user_symptom: New connections to databases in this region may have resulted in errors or timeouts
root_cause: An operator error led to an incorrect action being performed in close sequence on all four persisted metadata caches

user_symptom: Connectivity issues, failed connections, authentication failures, workspace login problems
root_cause: Procedural error during gateway maintenance routing connections to incomplete gateway ring

user_symptom: packet drops, timeouts, and/or increased latency
root_cause: fiber cut caused by severe weather conditions in the Netherlands

user_symptom: service degradation and increased latency for resources utilizing Azure Storage, including failure of virtual machine disks
root_cause: Incorrect limits set on the scale unit, load balancing automation was not sufficiently spreading the load

user_symptom: intermittent periods of degraded connectivity when attempting to connect to Azure, Microsoft 365, and Xbox resources
root_cause: dual fiber path failure and router configuration issue that continued to advertise local prefixes

user_symptom: Multiple Azure services connection and management issues
root_cause: Misattribution of domain ownership and update of nameservers to inactive servers

user_symptom: greater than expected throttling, intermittent failures and degraded performance
root_cause: Configuration change in throttling algorithm with adverse effects on scale units

user_symptom: customers experienced failures when trying to access resources
root_cause: utility power outage and UPS system failures

user_symptom: DNS resolution failures when connecting to an Azure Storage account, failures with launching clusters and serverless compute resources
root_cause: an issue with DNS resolution for Azure Storage accounts

user_symptom: intermittent latency or issues connecting to resources
root_cause: Local activity causing packet corruption and a significant fiber-cut

user_symptom: intermittent connection errors, timeouts, or increased latency
root_cause: routine configuration change with erroneous configuration file

user_symptom: Issues performing resource management operations
root_cause: Latent code defect in Entra authentication preview feature

user_symptom: higher latency and packet loss for communications between services hosted in the Commercial cloud and the Azure China cloud
root_cause: increase in traffic between the China Telecom network and the Azure Commercial cloud

service_name: Azure Front Door and Azure CDN
user_symptom: increased latency and connection timeouts, connection failures
root_cause: software regression causing intermittent high CPU consumption

user_symptom: customers experienced failures when attempting to provision new resources
root_cause: latent performance bug in the Registration Authority (RA), inadvertently introduced in the past month

user_symptom: increased latency, packet loss, failed connections and authentication failures
root_cause: code defect in a version update of a component that controls network traffic routing between Azure regions

user_symptom: Intermittent failures in Access Panel functionality
root_cause: Code change bug and auto-scaling configuration failure during traffic increase

user_symptom: delays, timeouts, and failures which were visible for long running operations
root_cause: A code modification which started rolling out on 6 Jan 2022 exposed a latent defect in the infrastructure used to process long running operations

user_symptom: Authentication errors across multiple Microsoft services and Azure Active Directory integrated applications
root_cause: Service update deployed incorrectly, causing crash in Azure AD backend services

user_symptom: service management errors, delays, and/or timeouts, GET and PUT errors, network connectivity issue
root_cause: retry storm triggered by zonal failure, .Net CLR and Garbage Collector issue

user_symptom: customers may have received failure notifications or 404 errors when attempting to create new resources through the Azure Portal
root_cause: a code change that was introduced which caused a key extension to fail, causing a null-reference within the Marketplace blade

user_symptom: intermittent errors when attempting to access the Azure portal and other Microsoft and Azure services
root_cause: code regression in a recent deployment to our edge DNS servers causing lock contention issues

user_symptom: intermittent connection failures and unexpected VM restarts
root_cause: OS update deployment task with code configuration error

user_symptom: Customers received 'AADSTS90033: A Transient error has occurred' error
root_cause: Unexpected increase in traffic volume and resource utilization exceeding operational thresholds

user_symptom: customers experienced errors when using Azure Resource Manager to perform service management operations
root_cause: A synchronization issue occurred between backend components... A configuration change was applied to these backend components, which resulted in some instances of the ARM service becoming unreachable

user_symptom: customers may have experienced issues accessing services and failed requests with increased latencies
root_cause: code bug in a performance optimization feature during network maintenance

user_symptom: customers may have encountered failures while attempting to authenticate
root_cause: an underlying code defect triggered some service nodes to experience a shortage of outbound ports to complete network tasks

user_symptom: intermittent errors when attempting to access the Azure portal and other Microsoft and Azure services
root_cause: Configuration issue with backend storage connection after maintenance

user_symptom: Sign-in issues when accessing Azure, Dynamics 365, and Microsoft 365 Services
root_cause: A regular OS update deployed to backend service endpoints caused unexpected service unresponsiveness

user_symptom: Failure notifications when performing service management operations on Windows VMs
root_cause: ARM template migration issue causing VM Agent extension visibility problem

user_symptom: Service management operations including create, rename, update and delete may have been impacted
root_cause: A manual maintenance operation impacted instances of an internal cluster data service

user_symptom: intermittently received errors when performing service management operations
root_cause: request queue limit on some frontends for disk management reached their limits and were rejecting a subset of requests

user_symptom: failure notifications when performing service management operations, errors when running pipelines and job executions, errors when attempting to provision SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS) and Integration Runtime
root_cause: During a scale-up operation of the service that manages customer network resources, a new capacity configuration was deployed. This configuration triggered a conflict with an existing backend service configuration

user_symptom: Service management and authentication failures
root_cause: OCSP and CRL endpoints not reachable due to telecom provider network issues

user_symptom: Token request failures and management operation failures
root_cause: Unsuccessful service deployment causing key rollover issues

user_symptom: Customers received HTTP status code 502 or 504
root_cause: Configuration change and traffic surge exceeding operational thresholds

user_symptom: errors and timeouts when attempting to sign in or sign up
root_cause: significant increase in workload during a planned maintenance operation

root_cause: a configuration change was implemented to create a redundant Domain Name Resolution zone for App Service SCM endpoints... due to a misconfiguration

root_cause: improperly encoded configuration file deployed with a bug in health notifications

root_cause: Backend service unhealthy due to unexpected traffic spike during hardware maintenance

root_cause: a subset of storage resources experienced a drop in network connectivity

root_cause: a backend network device became unhealthy, and traffic was not automatically rerouted
